adrian belew: Adrian Belew is an American guitarist, singer, and songwriter born in Kentucky in 1949. He gained recognition working with Frank Zappa, David Bowie, Talking Heads, and King Crimson, becoming known for his innovative guitar effects and unconventional playing techniques.
aereogramme: Aereogramme was a Scottish alternative rock band formed in Glasgow in 1998, featuring members Craig B., Iain Cook, and Campbell McNeil. The group was known for their dynamic sound that incorporated elements of post-rock, metal, and experimental rock.
aim: Aim is the stage name of Andrew Turner, a British electronic music producer from Derby, England who began releasing music in the mid-1990s. He became known for his sample-based trip hop and downtempo productions that blend jazz, soul, and hip-hop influences.
air: Air is a French electronic duo formed in Versailles in 1995, consisting of Nicolas Godin and Jean-Benoît Dunckel. The pair met in architecture school and became known for their dreamy, cinematic approach to electronic music that incorporates vintage synthesizers and live instrumentation.
allmyfriendsaresynths: allmyfriendsaresynths is an electronic music project that emerged in the mid-2020s, known for creating ambient and experimental soundscapes using primarily synthesized instruments. The artist maintains anonymity and releases music independently through digital platforms.
american hi-fi: American Hi-Fi is an American rock band formed in Boston, Massachusetts in 1998 by singer-guitarist Stacy Jones, formerly the drummer for Letters to Cleo. The band became known for their melodic pop-punk sound and radio-friendly alternative rock singles.
anamanaguchi: Anamanaguchi is a four-piece band from New York City formed in 2004, consisting of Peter Berkman, Ary Warnaar, James DeVito, and Luke Silas. They're known for combining traditional rock instrumentation with sounds generated from modified Nintendo Game Boy and NES hardware.
and you will know us by the trail of dea: And You Will Know Us by the Trail of Dead is an alternative rock band formed in Austin, Texas in 1994 by Conrad Keely and Jason Reece. The duo are known for their intense live performances, multi-instrumental abilities, and for both handling vocals and drums interchangeably.
andrew howie: Andrew Howie is a Scottish singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist from Glasgow. He has released several solo albums since the early 2010s, performing intimate folk and indie rock compositions.
animal collective: Animal Collective is an experimental pop band from Baltimore, Maryland, formed in 2003 by childhood friends Noah Lennox (Panda Bear), Dave Portner (Avey Tare), Josh Dibb (Deakin), and Brian Weitz (Geologist). The group is known for their layered vocal harmonies, electronic manipulation, and shifting lineup configurations across different projects.
antiproduct: AntiProduct was an American industrial metal band formed in the late 1990s. The group was known for combining heavy guitar riffs with electronic elements and aggressive vocals in the industrial rock tradition.
aphex twin: Aphex Twin is the primary alias of Richard D. James, an electronic music producer from Cornwall, England who began releasing music in the early 1990s. He is known for his complex programming, unconventional time signatures, and prolific output across multiple aliases including AFX and Polygon Window.
apollo 440: Apollo 440 are a British electronic music group formed in Liverpool in 1990, consisting of brothers Trevor and Howard Gray along with Noko and James Gardner. They're known for combining rock instrumentation with electronic beats and sampling techniques, achieving mainstream success in the late 1990s.
arcade fire: Arcade Fire is an indie rock band formed in Montreal, Quebec in 2001 by husband and wife Win Butler and Régine Chassagne. The group is known for their orchestral arrangements, anthemic songs, and theatrical live performances featuring multiple band members switching between various instruments.
archie bronson outfit: Archie Bronson Outfit is a three-piece rock band from London, England, formed in 2002. The group consists of Sam Windett, Mark Cleveland, and Dorian Hobday, known for their stripped-down approach to blues and country-influenced rock music.
art brut: Art Brut is a British indie rock band formed in London in 2003, fronted by vocalist Eddie Argos. The band is known for their witty, conversational lyrics and energetic live performances that often blur the line between singing and spoken word.
ash: Ash is a rock band from Downpatrick, Northern Ireland, formed in 1992 by Tim Wheeler, Mark Hamilton, and Rick McMurray. The trio became known for their energetic blend of punk, pop, and alternative rock throughout the 1990s and 2000s.
attack release: Attack Release is an electronic music project founded by producer and sound designer Marcus Fischer in Portland, Oregon in the early 2010s. The project focuses on creating ambient and experimental electronic compositions using field recordings, analog synthesizers, and digital processing techniques.
auf der mar: Auf der Mar is the solo project of Melissa Auf der Maur, a Canadian bassist and singer who played with Hole and The Smashing Pumpkins in the 1990s. She was born in Montreal and became known for her work in alternative rock bands before pursuing solo material.
balkan beat box: Balkan Beat Box is an electronic music group formed in 2003 by Israeli musician Tamir Muskat and American saxophonist Ori Kaplan. The band blends traditional Balkan folk music with electronic beats, hip-hop, and reggae influences.
barratt band: Barratt Band is a musical group with limited publicly available information about their formation, members, or origin. The band's background and career details remain largely undocumented in standard music references.
batteries: Batteries are a British pop-punk band formed in London in 2012. The group is known for their melodic approach to punk rock and energetic live performances.
battles: Battles is an experimental rock band formed in New York City in 2002 by guitarist/keyboardist Ian Williams and drummer John Stanier, later joined by guitarist Dave Konopka. The group is known for their intricate, math-rock influenced compositions that blend live instrumentation with electronic processing and looping techniques.
bay city rollers: Bay City Rollers were a Scottish pop rock band formed in Edinburgh in 1966. They became one of the biggest teen sensations of the mid-1970s, known for their tartan-clad image and catchy pop songs that sparked "Rollermania" across the UK and beyond.
beach boys: The Beach Boys formed in Hawthorne, California in 1961, consisting of brothers Brian, Dennis, and Carl Wilson, their cousin Mike Love, and friend Al Jardine. The group became known for their intricate vocal harmonies, innovative studio techniques, and Brian Wilson's sophisticated songwriting and production work.
beak>: Beak> is an English experimental rock band formed in Bristol in 2009, consisting of Geoff Barrow (Portishead), Billy Fuller, and Will Young. The trio creates instrumental music using analog synthesizers, drum machines, and guitars, often recorded live to tape.
